entries.answer_stated: theta = 180/n for an integer n >= 2 (win within n-1 moves)
entries.justification: Complete proof, with the cut lemma fully proved in the cited lemmas/cut-geometry.md (angle chase plus IVT for realizability). Sufficiency uses the round-up distances d(x): Claim 1 (sum of d's is theta or 2theta) and Claim 2 (existence of u, v with d(u) < v, with the n = 2 equality case correctly eliminated) give a legal cut making both halves marked, then the halving lemma descends to theta within n-1 moves total. Necessity is a clean exhaustive three-case maintenance argument for the no-marked-angle invariant from an equilateral start, where the only failure mode requires 180 = (j+k)theta. I checked every case and inequality; the argument is sound, and the exact-arithmetic computational verification is a bonus.

entries.answer_stated: theta = 180/n for an integer n >= 2 (win within n-1 cuts)
entries.justification: Complete and self-contained proof. Lemma 0 gives the cut parametrization with realizability; the chain lemma and the double-threat Lemma 2 (least k with C < k*theta < 180 - B, with a separate correct n = 2 argument showing B, C < 90) prove sufficiency within n-1 cuts, and the four-case closure Lemma 4 plus an explicit safe-start construction prove necessity. I verified the strictness chain k*theta <= C + theta < C + A (using A > theta, which is properly established from A >= 60 >= theta and A not in theta*N) and found the case analyses exhaustive. No gaps of substance.

Complete proof in compact form. Sufficiency: the relabeling lemma (integer k with b < k < a+b, all-below-1 case correctly forced to N = 2) gives a legal cut planting integral angles N-k and k in the two children, followed by the theta/(m-1)theta descent. Necessity: in G = R/(theta Z) with t = [180] != 0, the equilateral start is safe and the four pairings (u = v = 0; u = b + u = 0; v = c + v = 0; c + v = b + u = 0 forcing t = 0) exhaust the ways both children could be unsafe. The cut mechanics and crossbar-type legality are asserted with one-line angle-sum justifications rather than derived at length, which is acceptable; the argument is correct and both directions are fully proved.
key_issues: Cut legality (interior ray meets opposite side) asserted rather than proved; standard fact, minor blemish

entries.justification: Complete proof of both directions, verified step by step. The cut model (1)-(2) is correct with the legality criterion 0 < x < A. Lemma 1 (angle m*theta => win in <= m-1 cuts) is a clean induction with the theta + (m-1)theta split. Sufficiency: the relabeling Claim (integer k with b < k < a+b, where the all-below-1 case correctly forces n = 2 via a+b = 2-c > 1, and 1 <= k <= n-1 is derived from positivity and k < n-c) yields the cut x = (k-b)theta planting the integral cut-point angles k*theta and (n-k)*theta in the two children, so Lemma 1 finishes against any retention. Necessity: 'safe' invariant with an exhaustive four-case analysis (cases 1-3 contradict parent safety via A, B, C respectively; case 4 forces 180 = (p+q)theta), equilateral safe start, so Shan-Yu survives forever. All positivity/legality checks present.
key_issues: Realizability of the cut (ray from the vertex meets the opposite side) asserted in one line, as in the default run; standard fact, cosmetic only
